Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: MCP improvements and parser fixes — ALP-798 to ALP-803 (#69)

* nancy[ALP-798]: remove fmm_file_info dead alias

- Removed fmm_file_info Tool block from handle_tools_list()
- Removed "fmm_file_info" dispatch arm from call_tool()
- Updated fmm_file_metadata legacy alias to point to tool_file_outline
- Removed tool_file_info() method (was a one-liner delegating to tool_file_outline)
- Updated fmm_file_outline description — removed "fmm_file_info is an alias" note
- Updated unit test in mod.rs: fmm_file_info → fmm_file_outline
- Renamed integration test file_info_delegates_to_file_outline → file_outline_returns_symbols
  and updated it to call fmm_file_outline directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-799]: document fmm_glossary dotted-pattern contract in MCP description

- Updated fmm_glossary tool description to explain the two modes:
  - Bare name (e.g. 'loadInstance') → file-level used_by for all files importing the symbol's file
  - Dotted name (e.g. 'Injector.loadInstance') → call-site precision via second tree-sitter pass
- Updated 'pattern' property description in input_schema with same distinction
- Mode parameter (source/tests/all) descriptions left as-is (already documented)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-800]: document fmm_search depends_on transitive behavior

- Updated fmm_search tool description to note that depends_on uses transitive
  matching (full import chain), not direct-only; contrast with fmm_dependency_graph depth=1
- Updated depends_on property description with the same distinction and a
  concrete recommendation: "for direct-only importers, use fmm_dependency_graph with depth=1"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-801]: fix local dep resolution for directory-style JS/TS imports

Root cause: try_resolve_local_dep() had no index-file fallback for JS/TS relative
paths. `./module` → `module.ts` worked (stem comparison via dep_matches), but
`./module` → `module/index.ts` failed silently, leaving real files in `external`.

Fix: two-pass resolution in try_resolve_local_dep() for `./` and `../` paths:
- First pass: existing dep_matches scan (direct file, extension-agnostic)
- Second pass: explicit index-file candidates (index.ts, index.tsx, index.js, index.jsx)
  tried only when the first pass finds nothing. This guarantees direct file
  takes priority over index-file variant when both exist in the manifest.

Downstream detection: added dep_targets_file() helper that delegates relative
imports to try_resolve_local_dep() (which includes index-file fallback), and
non-relative imports to dep_matches/python_dep_matches. Used in:
- dependency_graph() downstream filter
- dependency_graph_transitive() downstream seed + BFS inner loop
- search_files() depends_on filter

Result: no index check in dep_matches (clean, no priority ambiguity). All
downstream detection paths use the same resolution logic as upstream.

Tests: 15 new unit tests in search::tests covering:
- index.ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx variants of directory-style imports
- direct file priority over index.ts when both exist
- extensionless imports resolving .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx
- parent-relative (../) resolution to direct file and index file
- deep nesting with excess .. clamped to root
- unresolvable relative path stays in external
- wrong-directory index.ts is not matched
- downstream detection for directory-style imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-802]: fix fmm_read_symbol truncation marker to reference truncate: false

Changed the marker text in cap_response() from "Use more specific filters."
to "Use truncate: false to get the full source." — the former is search tool
advice; the latter is the correct action for symbol reads where the bypass
parameter is already implemented and documented.

Updated the test to assert the new marker text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* nancy[ALP-803]: fmm_list_files — add sort_by and order parameters

Added sort_by (name|loc|exports) and order (asc|desc) parameters to
fmm_list_files with smart defaults:
- Omitted → sort_by=name, order=asc (backwards-compatible)
- sort_by=loc or sort_by=exports without order → desc (largest first)
- sort_by=name without order → asc (alphabetical)

Changes:
- ListFilesArgs: added sort_by and order fields
- Tool schema: added sort_by and order with enum constraints and descriptions
- Tool description: updated to mention sort_by capability
- tool_list_files(): validation (invalid values return clear errors), sort dispatch
- 7 unit tests covering: default alphabetical, loc desc/asc, exports desc,
  name desc, invalid sort_by error, invalid order error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix: Agregar botón "Cerrar" manual en ventana de progreso (mode config)

Problema:
- En mode config, al dar guardar se mostraba "sincronizado"
- Pero la ventana se cerraba automáticamente después de 1 segundo
- El usuario no tenía tiempo de ver los contadores y porcentajes

Solución implementada:
- Agregar botón "Cerrar" en la ventana de progreso
- Botón inicialmente deshabilitado con texto "⏳ Sincronizando..."
- Cuando termina la sincronización:
  * Se detiene la barra de progreso
  * Se actualiza el estado a "✅ Completado"
  * Se habilita el botón con texto "✅ Cerrar" (verde)
- Eliminar cierre automático de 1 segundo
- Usuario decide cuándo cerrar la ventana

Cambios:
- Aumentar tamaño de ventana a 750x650 para el botón
- Crear función cerrar_ventana() que:
  * Cierra ventana de progreso
  * Destruye ventana de configuración
  * Inicia System Tray
- Marcar sincronización como completada (sync_completada flag)
- Eliminar código duplicado de self.root.destroy()

Ahora el usuario puede ver los contadores y porcentajes tanto tiempo
como quiera antes de cerrar la ventana manualmente.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add stderr support and lines/stderrLines Stream bridges to beamtalk_subprocess.erl (BT-1130) (#1182)

## Summary

Part of [BT-1130](https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1130) — ADR 0051
Phase 4b.

Extends the `beamtalk_subprocess` gen_server with:

- **stderr support**: Independent `readStderrLine` / `readStderrLine:`
selectors with separate buffer, pending, and waiting state — symmetric
with stdout
- **Stream bridges**: `lines` and `stderrLines` selectors returning
Streams whose generators call `gen_server:call` from the caller's
process (ADR 0021 pattern)
- **Bug fix**: Changed `reply_to_waiter` to use tagged `{timer, From,
TimerRef}` tuple to disambiguate from `gen_server:From = {Pid, Ref}` —
the previous `{From, TimerRef}` pattern was ambiguous and caused
`function_clause` errors in `gen:reply`

## Key Changes

- `beamtalk_subprocess.erl`: stderr state in init, 6 new handle_call
clauses, stderr routing in handle_info, exit/close flush both channels,
`make_readline_stream/2` + `make_readline_gen/2` helpers
- `beamtalk_subprocess_tests.erl`: 6 new EUnit tests (stderr EOF,
timeout, channel independence, lines Stream, stderrLines Stream)

## Test Plan

- [x] `readStderrLine` returns stderr data and nil at EOF
- [x] `readStderrLine:` with timeout returns nil when no data
- [x] stdout and stderr buffers are independent
- [x] `lines` Stream yields correct stdout line sequence
- [x] `stderrLines` Stream yields correct stderr line sequence
- [x] `just ci` passes (all 2927 runtime tests, 814 BUnit tests, E2E
tests)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added stderr channel support, enabling independent handling of
standard error alongside standard output.
* Added line-based reading operations for both stdout and stderr
streams.
* Introduced stream-based reading capabilities for line-oriented data
retrieval.

* **Tests**
* Expanded test coverage for stderr reading, stream operations, and
stdout/stderr buffer independence.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Raise threshold from 0.15 to 0.40 (genuine matches score 0.4+)
- Make jersey prompts text-specific instead of generic "brand on sportswear"
- Remove overly broad "logo or text" fallback prompt

Performance Optimization with Virtualized Email Lists (#57)

* auto-claude: subtask-1-1 - Add paginated getEmails query with LIMIT/OFFSET, total count, and category counts

- Add getEmailsPaginated() with LIMIT/OFFSET and snippet field (first 150 chars of body)
- Add getEmailCount() for total email count per account
- Add getCategoryCounts() for DB-level category count aggregation
- Add composite index on (accountId, folder, isRead) for efficient unread counts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-1-2 - Expose paginated DB functions via IPC

Add IPC handlers for getEmailsPaginated, getEmailCount, and
getCategoryCounts in main.cjs, preload.cjs, and electron.d.ts.
Existing getEmails handler preserved for backward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-2-1 - Install react-virtuoso as a production dependency.

* auto-claude: subtask-3-1 - Refactor useEmails to use backend counts and remove client pagination

- Add totalCount field from getEmailCount backend API
- Add backendCategoryCounts from getCategoryCounts backend API
- Remove visibleCount state (virtualization handles display)
- displayedEmails = filteredEmails (no more client-side slicing)
- canLoadMore = false (virtualization handles rendering)
- loadMoreEmails becomes no-op for backward compatibility
- Keep all existing filtering/sorting logic for compatibility
- Maintain full backward-compatible return interface

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-3-2 - Update App.tsx to use paginated email loading

Replace getEmails() with getEmailsPaginated() for initial load, account
switching, sync completion, and add account flows. Wire up totalCount
and resetPagination from refactored useEmails hook. Add totalCount prop
to EmailList interface for upcoming virtualization work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-4-1 - Refactor EmailList to use react-virtuoso Virtuoso component

Replace slice/map rendering with virtualized list for performance.
Extract memoized EmailRow sub-component. Use endReached for infinite
scroll. Show totalCount in header instead of loaded emails count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-5-1 - Update EmailList.test.tsx to work with react-virtuoso

Mock react-virtuoso's Virtuoso component to render items directly in JSDOM.
Update Load More tests to use endReached callback pattern. Update Virtual
Scrolling tests for totalCount display, endReached firing, and loading state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-5-2 - Update useEmails.test.ts for the refactored hook.

- Mock new IPC methods (getEmailCount, getCategoryCounts, searchEmails)
- Update pagination tests to reflect virtualization (displayedEmails = filteredEmails, canLoadMore always false, loadMoreEmails is no-op)
- Add tests for totalCount tracking from backend
- Add tests for category switching triggering new backend queries
- Add tests for re-fetching counts on account switch and email changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add LRU cache (last 10 viewed) for email body content in useEmails
- Evict body/bodyHtml from emails pushed out of cache when selecting new emails
- Strip all body content from previous account's emails when switching accounts
- Clear body content from non-selected emails when switching folders/categories

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* auto-claude: subtask-7-1 - Run full test suite and fix lint issues

Remove unused PAGE_SIZE constant from useEmails hook. Fix prettier
formatting issues across source files. All 1152 tests pass, TypeScript
compiles cleanly, and build succeeds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(critical): applySuggestion usa supabaseAdmin para garantizar TRAMITE

Bug: getTramiteOperacion usaba el client del usuario (con RLS) que
posiblemente no encontraba la escritura TRAMITE. Los participantes
del apunte terminaban en INGESTA (antecedente) contaminándolo.

Fix:
- getTramiteOperacion ahora usa supabaseAdmin (bypasea RLS)
- Insert de participantes y update de operaciones usan supabaseAdmin
- Logging detallado: lista todas las escrituras antes de operar
- Double-check: verifica source de la escritura post-operación
- Migración 046: mover participantes manuales de INGESTA a TRAMITE
  (detecta origen_dato != 'IA_OCR' para distinguir manual vs ingest)

EJECUTAR migración 046 en Supabase SQL Editor.
Luego REINICIAR el servidor de desarrollo (npm run dev).

commit.message:
Add Subprocess.bt class file and register as built-in actor (BT-1131) (#1181)

## Summary

- Create `stdlib/src/Subprocess.bt` — Actor subclass with class factory
methods and @primitive instance method stubs
- Register `Subprocess` in
`beamtalk_class_hierarchy_table:all_builtins/0`
- Auto-generated: `generated_builtins.rs` and `beamtalk_stdlib.app.src`

## Details

Phase 4c of ADR 0051 (Subprocess Execution). Adds the Beamtalk-facing
class definition for `Subprocess`:

- **Class methods:** `open:args:` and `open:args:env:dir:` (desugar to
`spawnWith:`)
- **Instance methods (primitives):** `writeLine:`, `readLine`,
`readLine:`, `readStderrLine`, `readStderrLine:`, `lines`,
`stderrLines`, `exitCode`, `close`
- **No state declarations** — state lives in the hand-written
`beamtalk_subprocess.erl` gen_server

Linear: https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1131

## Test plan

- [x] `just build` passes
- [x] `just test` passes (814 BUnit + 2922 EUnit)
- [x] `just clippy` passes
- [x] `just fmt-check` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

commit.message:
fix: move @vuepoint/bridge to devDependencies in vue package

Bridge code is fully bundled into @vuepoint/vue at build time (zero
imports of @vuepoint/bridge in dist/index.js). Having it as a runtime
dependency caused pnpm to try resolving it, failing because there's
no bridge tarball on the release. Moving to devDependencies means it's
only needed during monorepo development, not by consumers.

Fix optimistic rollback, suppress race conditions, and type safety

  and shows error toast instead of silently failing
- Replace fragile setTimeout-based suppress pattern with async-aware
  suppressDuring() helper that ties suppression to actual operation
  completion (both board and backlog contexts)
- Guard weekly transition against multi-tab race — set localStorage
  marker before running transition, re-check before starting
- Remove `as any` casts in clients.ts, use proper Supabase types
- Fix malformed indentation in clients.ts

Fix fdata2basis_2d/basis2fdata_2d name collision with extendr wrappers

The extendr-wrappers.R file defined fdata2basis_2d and basis2fdata_2d

Rename the raw bindings to *_raw to avoid the collision.

commit.message:
feat: EAV triple store & Graph RAG integration into curation pipeline

## sinain-koog — new modules
- triplestore.py: SQLite-backed immutable EAV triple store with 4 covering
  indexes (EAVT, AEVT, VAET, AVET), transactional assertions, logical
  retraction, and GC for facts older than configurable threshold
- triple_extractor.py: 3-tier extraction (JSON direct → regex → LLM fallback)
  converts signals, playbook entries, session summaries, and mining findings
  into typed entity-attribute-value triples
- triple_ingest.py: CLI entry point for fire-and-forget ingestion from plugin;
  supports signal, session, mining, playbook, module activate/retract modes
- triple_migrate.py: schema migration runner (forward-only, idempotent)
- triple_query.py: retrieval interface — semantic search + graph expansion
  returns formatted context string for injection into agent prompts
- embedder.py: sentence-transformers wrapper with numpy cosine similarity
  for semantic triple retrieval

## sinain-koog — updates
- koog-config.json: add triple_extractor model config and triplestore settings
  (dbPath, maxTriplesPerTx, conceptExtractionMode, gcOlderThanDays)
- memory_miner.py: inject graph context from triple_query before LLM mining
- module_manager.py: fire-and-forget ingest on activate; retract on suspend
- requirements.txt: add numpy, sentence-transformers

## sinain-hud-plugin
- Inject KNOWLEDGE GRAPH CONTEXT into agent context on each heartbeat
  (triple_query.py, 10s timeout, skipped silently on failure)
- Fire-and-forget triple_ingest after: signal analysis, session summary write,
  memory mining, playbook curation, and module activation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical security vulnerabilities from penetration test audit

- C1: Add authMiddleware to face /status, /register, /verify endpoints
  with ownership checks (students can only access own face data)
- C2: Move face routes after CSRF middleware; remove face CSRF exemption
- C3: Add tenant_id filtering to all lecturer data queries (students,
  dashboard-stats, exports, login-history, face-status, fraud, audit)
- C3: Add requirePermission('manage_tenants') to tenant CRUD routes
- H2: Reject client 'system' role in chatbot; inject hardcoded system prompt
- H3: Add CSV formula injection prevention (escapeCsvField) to exports
- H4: Split body size limits: 10MB for /api/face, 1MB default for rest
- H5: Replace in-memory rate limits/lockout with SQLite-backed tables
- M2: Invalidate all sessions on password change (tokens_invalidated_at)
- M5: Replace silent catch {} with console.warn for permission loading
- Frontend: Add fraud flags UI + anti-cheat hook integration

commit.message:
Dark theme, meetup coordination, and deploy prep

- Full dark theme across all pages and components
  (dark-950 bg, dark-800 cards, dark-700 elevated, emerald accents)
- Meetup coordination page (/meetups) for organizing group outings
  with spots tracker, attendee avatars, join/leave, weather preview
- meetups + meetup_attendees tables with RLS policies
- Meetups nav link added

repository.description: AI-powered Python plotting gallery. One spec, nine libraries — matplotlib, plotly, seaborn, bokeh, altair & more. Compare and copy working code.

commit.message:
feat: comprehensive test suite (180 tests) + security hardening + service categories

- Add 12 new test files covering API routes, business logic, validation, cache, middleware, and UI components
- Fix inventory check-services test with proper withTenant and kit-preparation mocks
- Implement withTenant wrapper for all ~182 API routes (tenant enforcement)
- Add Redis cache layer with SCAN, error resilience, and graceful fallback
- Wire Sentry instrumentation (server + edge), withSentryConfig in next.config
- Harden Docker: wget healthcheck, Redis depends_on, non-root runner
- Reduce DB pool max 60→30, remove CSP unsafe-eval
- Add service categories with icons, reorder, and analytics
- Update vitest config with pool: "forks" for test isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add address autocomplete with debounced Nominatim suggestions

Fetches location suggestions as the user types (debounced 300ms),
displays them in an accessible dropdown with keyboard navigation,
and skips the geocode call on submit when a suggestion is selected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
